source: Confluence-distilled BMW pipeline diagnostics with BMW Git source citations where available.
operator_facing_wording_rule: Surface candidate hypotheses and recommended actions. Never mark missing actual images as passed.

patterns.scope: BMW pipeline screenshot test actual/diff image
patterns.trigger_description: Operator opens a screenshot review row and the actual or diff image shows pronounced magenta or pink tint on the rendered model surface or fills the frame.
trigger_signals: Predominant color in actual image lies in magenta/pink range (R ~ B, G low) across the car body or full frame
Diff image highlights the magenta region as a high-delta cluster
Visual classification today flagged this as missing_candidate or unclear_manual_review

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: An external resource the scene depends on (HDRI environment, texture, color reference, asset binary) is not correctly linked at render time. Graphics pipelines conventionally render magenta as the fallback color for an unbound texture sampler.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Check the .exr (HDRI) linkage in the scene's source file. For Blender masterfiles, switch to the Shading workspace, choose World in the Shader Editor dropdown, and verify the orange texture node points at the correct city.exr. Relink via the folder icon if needed. For RaCo scenes the equivalent step is to verify the texture/material assignment in the Resources panel.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote: The object lighting is driven by the external hdri file "city.exr" which should be found in a folder next to the masterfile. In case of visual errors in the scene, for example if the car mesh has a magenta tint, make sure to check if the .exr file is still correctly linked.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.evidence_strength: documented; OldOutdated section so context is Blender masterfile specifically; the broader graphics convention (unbound texture renders magenta) is industry-standard and transfers

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: For Ambient Layer screenshot tests on non-Rolls-Royce brands, full or half pink fill is the expected output for the Intensity Zero and Intensity Half test classes. The test is designed to show the pink underlay when Main_Intensity is 0 or 0.5 and the Neutral Background overlay is not active.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Before diagnosing as a defect, check the test name. If it is the AL Intensity Zero or Intensity Half test on BMW / BMW_M / Alpina (not RR), the pink fill is the documented expected output. The actual image only diverges if the expected and actual disagree on the same pink region.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote_1:
Intensity Zero — This test shows the result of setting Main_Intensity to 0
In the case of Rolls Royce CIDs, the Neutral Background is expected to be seen instead of the full pink color.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote_2:
Intensity Half — This test shows the result of setting Main_Intensity to 0.5
In the case of Rolls Royce CIDs, the Neutral Background is expected to be half-seen instead of the half pink color.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.evidence_strength: documented; AL-specific; does NOT apply to 3D Car body renders

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX surfaces both hypotheses with their Confluence anchor links; operator selects which applies after inspecting the test name + the scene's source file linkage.
bmw_git_source_citations: ci/scripts/asset_testing/testing_utils.py: passes screenshots_clear_color to the viewer --clear argument
ci/scripts/asset_testing/LUA/runTests.lua: writes one actual PNG per enabled screenshot test

patterns.scope: BMW pipeline screenshot test produced no diff PNG for a test that has an expected PNG
patterns.trigger_description: After a screenshot test run, the cars/BMW/<profile>/export/tests/diff/ folder has no <test>_color.png even though the matching expected/ entry exists. Tests appear to have run but diffs were never written.
trigger_signals: diff/ folder empty or partial after run
Tests appear to have executed (exit 0 from script)
expected/ folder fully populated
actuals/ folder may also be empty

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: The screenshot-test script requires the -d / --diff flag since 2023-11-27 to actually save diff PNGs. Without it, the script tests and reports failed tests but does not persist the diff images.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Re-run with the -d (or --diff) argument: `py main.py screenshots_ext C:/PATH/TO/PROJECT.rca -b BMW -d`. SGFX's wrapper should already inject this flag; if it is missing, this is an SGFX bug worth filing.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote: Note: As of 27. Nov 2023, "-d" (or "--diff") is required as an additional argument for the script to actually save the screenshot diffs, otherwise it will just test and report failed tests

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: First-time screenshot generation needs the -g / --generate-screenshots flag because the expected/ folder is empty and there is nothing to compare against. Subsequent diff runs do not.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: If expected/ for this car is empty or sparse, re-run with -g: `py main.py screenshots_ext C:/PATH/TO/PROJECT.rca -b BMW -g`. Then run a normal pass to populate diff/.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote: If you want to generate new screenshots altogether (e.g. for a first test), add "-g" or "--generate-screenshorts" as arguments

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: IDC23 cars need the assets/idc23 branch checked out. As of 2024-09-10 the CI on master does not work for IDC23 cars.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: If diagnosing an IDC23 car (U06, U10, U11, U12 family), check that the BMW Git checkout is on assets/idc23. If on master, switch: `git checkout assets/idc23 && git pull`. SGFX read-only checks should already flag this — confirm SGFX's lane detector classified the car correctly.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote: Note: As of 10. Sep 2024, the ci on the master branch doesn't work for IDC23 cars. Use the assets/idc23 branch instead.

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX surfaces the three candidate hypotheses + flag-injection trace + branch detection result; operator confirms before re-running.

patterns.scope: Generated actual screenshots have missing strips of pixels or test fails despite matching content
patterns.trigger_description: Generated screenshots are cropped relative to expected. Tests fail with what looks like content drift but the underlying cause is the renderer was forced into a smaller window than expected.
trigger_signals: Actual image width or height smaller than expected image
Visible pixel-strip gaps when inspecting in Photoshop or image viewer
Failures concentrated at one edge of the frame
Operator runs on single small monitor or laptop screen

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: BMW screenshot tests generate images sized to monitor resolution. Below 3340 width or 1440 height, output is cropped and the comparison against expected fails. In multi-monitor setups widths add; heights take the max.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Check the operator workstation's monitor resolution. If below 3340 × 1440 single-monitor, attach a second monitor (widths add) or use a higher-resolution display. SGFX should detect this from a Win32 GetSystemMetrics call before running screenshot tests and warn the operator.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote: The screenshots are generated based on the monitor resolution on your pc. You should make sure that you have at least 3340 x 1440 resolution, otherwise the generated screenshots will be cropped and the tests will fail. If you have multiple monitors connected, the resolution adds up on the width (you need to have monitor1.width + monitor2.width > 3340) but not on the height (for the height you need at least one of the monitors to have the height > 1440). To make sure the screenshots generated properly, you can put the image into photoshop and see if there are any missing strips of pixels.

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX can pre-check operator resolution and warn before running; operator decides whether to proceed or escalate to ops for a hardware upgrade.

patterns.scope: Expected tests are not running or new tests added in BMW Git are not picked up by SGFX
patterns.trigger_description: A test the operator expects to see in the diff queue is absent from the actual/diff output, or a test SGFX flags as missing was deliberately disabled per car.
trigger_signals: Expected test name in BMW common.lua does not appear in actual/diff output
Operator reports a previously-existing test no longer runs
test_config.lua content includes disableTests() entries that match the missing test

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: Per-car test config explicitly disables this test via disableTests() in cars/BMW/<profile>/export/tests/test_config.lua. This is a deliberate exclusion, not a defect.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Open cars/BMW/<profile>/export/tests/test_config.lua and search for disableTests("<test_name>") or the test's identifier. If it is intentionally disabled, no action — surface the disable line as evidence and move on. If unexpectedly disabled, escalate to data-prep or CI-team for a reason.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote: "./digital-3d-car/cars/BMW/U06/export/tests/test_config.lua" - with possibility to addTests()/disableTests() per car model

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: A new test was added via addTests() in test_config.lua locally but the BMW Git checkout was not refreshed (git pull) since.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Run `git fetch && git status` in the BMW Git checkout. If behind origin, escalate to operator confirmation gate for `git pull`. Per SVN-100-percent-gate this is BMW Git read-refresh which is OK; SGFX confirms with operator before pulling.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.evidence_strength: documented; mechanism inferred from canonical addTests/disableTests

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX reads test_config.lua and surfaces the disable/add entries; operator decides whether the configuration matches their expectation.

patterns.scope: BMW Git pull or fetch fails with cannot-lock-ref errors
patterns.trigger_description: Operator attempts git pull/fetch in C:/3D Car git/digital-3d-car-models and gets repeated `error: cannot lock ref` lines with hash mismatches.
trigger_signals: stderr contains 'error: cannot lock ref'
stderr contains '(unable to update local ref)'
stderr contains 'is at <sha> but expected <other-sha>'
git pull returns nonzero with branch refs partially updated

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: Local ref store has diverged from origin. Three documented recovery paths: hard reset + clean, delete the problematic branch, or garbage collect with prune.
operator_action_choices: Hard recovery: `git reset --hard && git clean -fxd` — destroys local changes; SGFX MUST get explicit operator confirmation before suggesting this and never auto-run.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote:
If you face something similar like the following:
error: cannot lock ref 'refs/remotes/origin/features/...' 
! ...  (unable to update local ref)
try to clean your local repo first with:
git reset --hard
git clean -fxd
then try the update.. 
or 
try to delete the problematic branch:
git branch --delete branchname
try then
git pull
or
try
git gc --prune=now
then try
git pull

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX MUST NOT auto-run `git reset --hard` — the operator confirmation gate is mandatory because reset --hard destroys uncommitted work. SGFX surfaces the three documented paths and lets the operator pick.

patterns.scope: Color token extractor output shows 'Anything else' line instead of Hex 6 / Hex 8 value
patterns.trigger_description: When the IDCevo color-token overview tool emits a JSON-key line without a Hex value, the underlying color reference could not be resolved or used an unimplemented modifier.
trigger_signals: Output line format <Full Json Key> [Anything else] instead of <Full Json Key> <Hex 6>
Operator reports CarPaint visual divergence between expected reference and rendered output

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: The color uses a reference to another color that cannot be resolved (e.g., references a token that no longer exists or was renamed).
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Trace the reference chain in the source JSON. If the referenced color is missing, the upstream change that removed it is the root cause — escalate to whoever owns that color token.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote:
Anything else — If the Color could not be resolved because of missing / unresolved reference Color
Or if the Color used modifiers / functions that are not implemented

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: The color uses a modifier or function the extractor does not implement (e.g., a custom color math).
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Inspect the source color definition for non-trivial modifier calls. Cross-check against the documented extractor capabilities.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote: Or if the Color used modifiers / functions that are not implemented

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX surfaces the unresolved-color lines + their reference chains; operator and CarPaint owner together decide.

patterns.scope: Operator or SGFX invokes the wrong script entry point for the car's lane
patterns.trigger_description: IDC23 cars driven through car_manager.py or IDC_EVO cars driven through main.py — each lane has its canonical entry point.
trigger_signals: Script exits nonzero immediately on a known-good profile
Error refers to a model not in the script's registry
Profile-action combination known to work in other operator's lane fails on this one

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: IDCEVO cars must use car_manager.py, not main.py.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Re-invoke with `py car_manager.py screenshots <profile>` for IDC_EVO profiles. SGFX's lane-aware router (Phase G-4 fec3a07) should already pick this correctly — confirm SGFX's lane detector saw the profile as IDC_EVO.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote: Note: If you want to screenshottest for IDCEVO, the script to use is not main.py but car_manager.py.

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: For external invocation against an .rca file outside the BMW Git tree, the canonical entry is `py main.py screenshots_ext` with the -b BMW / -b MINI brand flag. The page explicitly notes this route is not recommended.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Prefer the in-tree invocation through the BMW Git checkout. Only fall back to screenshots_ext if there is no other path; even then prefer car_manager.py for IDC_EVO.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote:
For running the screenshot tests "externally" (e.g. in the SVN), instead run:
py main.py screenshots_ext C:/PATH/TO/PROJECT.rca -b BMW
This one is NOT recomended
"-b" is a required argument for the script to work; depending on the brand, specify either "-b BMW" or "-b MINI"

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX surfaces the lane-router decision and the canonical entry point; operator confirms before SGFX spawns the subprocess.

patterns.scope: Borderline screenshot test failures where the visual difference is small but exceeds the configured threshold
patterns.trigger_description: Tests fail with very low changed-pixel-ratio (under a few percent) and operator wants to investigate whether the tolerance is set correctly for this car class.
trigger_signals: Diff delta badge yellow or red with low percent value
Diff cluster scattered, no single high-intensity blob
Operator considers the change visually acceptable

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: The BLOCK_CONFIG_LIST in digital-3d-car/ci/scripts/test/image_cmp.py (lines 22-23) defines per-block tolerance bands. Operators can adjust these locally to investigate borderline failures, then revert.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Inspect image_cmp.py lines 22-23: `BLOCK_CONFIG_LIST = [(1, 0.1), (2, 0.05), (4, 0.01), (8, 0.005), (16, 0.001)]`. Each tuple is `(allowed max difference per pixel, allowed % of pixels)`. Adjust LOCALLY for investigation only; do not commit to BMW Git. If the change would help permanently, file a ticket through the data-prep team.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote:
Diff tolerance
You can locally adjust the comparison tolerance via digital-3d-car/ci/scripts/test/image_cmp.py (line 22,23):
#Blocks are defined as (allowed max difference per pixel, allowed % of pixels)
BLOCK_CONFIG_LIST = [(1, 0.1), (2, 0.05), (4, 0.01), (8, 0.005), (16, 0.001)]

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX surfaces the documented tolerance bands and the BMW Git read-only policy: any tolerance edit is local-only investigation, never committed by SGFX. BMW source READ-ONLY for writes per [[feedback-grade-production-alignment]].

patterns.scope: Operator stages .ramses / .logic / diff/ / actuals/ / _Workfiles files for commit
patterns.trigger_description: Files that should never enter BMW Git appear in git status as Modified or Added.
trigger_signals: git status shows *.ramses or *.logic
git status shows files under diff/ or actuals/ for a car
git status shows files under _Workfiles

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: These artifact families are excluded by the per-car .gitignore (e.g., digital-3d-car/cars/BMW/U11/export/.gitignore) and should never be staged. If git status shows them, either .gitignore was edited / removed, or the operator used `git add -f` to bypass it.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Unstage the files: `git restore --staged <file>`. Verify .gitignore is intact: `git ls-files --error-unmatch cars/BMW/<profile>/export/.gitignore`. SGFX should NEVER auto-commit BMW Git — this remediation is operator-only per the BMW Git read-only policy.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote_1: Never commit *.ramses or *.logic files (should be already excluded within each car folder via "./digital-3d-car/cars/BMW/U11/export/.gitignore")!
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote_2: Never commit diff/actual folders (should be already excluded within each car folder via "./digital-3d-car/cars/BMW/U11/export/.gitignore")!

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX surfaces the staged-but-excluded list as a hard warning before the operator pushes to BMW Git. BMW Git access is read-only for writes per [[feedback-grade-production-alignment]] — SGFX does NOT perform the restore, only flags.

patterns.scope: A previously-working car or scene visually degrades after a CarLib upgrade
patterns.trigger_description: Operator reports that a screenshot test that passed last week now fails or shows visual artifacts, correlated with a CarLib version bump in the downstream consumer app.
trigger_signals: Same scene + same RaCo version + new CarLib version = failing test
Bisecting CarLib versions narrows to a single transition (e.g., 15.10.0 ok / 15.12.0 broken)
Issue is visible in consumer-app context (Splitscreen ADV, Launcher ADV) not just the screenshot test

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: A specific CarLib version range introduced a behavioral change. Documented example: 15.10.0 worked, 15.12.0 did not, with 15.11.0 untested. Investigation may correlate with a specific change such as the LiveData unload behavior in IDCEVODEV-717751.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Surface the CarLib versions in the consumer app + the screenshot-test renderer. If a regression window exists, escalate to data-prep / CI team with the bisection range. Do not auto-rollback the CarLib version.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote:
We tried different CarLibs:
15.10.0 works
15.11.0 -> will be tested (we had issues to check it out) -> Can it be related to [IDCEVODEV-717751] [CarLib] Always unload LiveData when OnStopped - CodeCraft Jira?
15.12.0 -> does not work
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.evidence_strength: documented; one historical case, pattern is canonical but the specific versions are example data

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Manual review remains required. SGFX surfaces the version delta + the linked Jira ticket pattern; operator + data-prep team triage.

patterns.scope: Operator's first-time setup or post-reinstall: `pip3` (or `pip`) not on PATH
patterns.trigger_description: Operator runs `pip3 install -r ci/scripts/requirements/ipn.txt` and gets `'pip' is not recognized as an internal or external command`.
trigger_signals: pip3 / pip in PATH check returns nothing
Operator reports running the setup commands for the first time on this laptop

possible_causes_honest_disjunction.hypothesis: Python's Scripts directory (where pip / pip3 live) is not on the system PATH environment variable.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.operator_action: Type `start %appdata%` in cmd, go up one directory, navigate to Local/Programs/Python/Python39/Scripts, copy the address, add it to the Path system environment variable, restart cmd. Phase D should automate this — if Phase D is active, this hypothesis is moot.
possible_causes_honest_disjunction.verbatim_confluence_quote:
If pip3 and pip didn't worked ("'pip' is not recognized as an internal or external command") try this :
Type start %appdata% in cmd.
Go back one directory and navigate to Local/Programs/Python/Python39/Scripts.
After you do this you now have the correct location of your downloaded Python. Copy your file path by selecting the whole directory in the address bar.
Once you do that click the start icon and navigate to the Control Panel → System and Security → System. Then click "Advanced System Settings" on the left side of the panel.
Once there, click Environment Variables on the bottom right and there will be two boxes, an upper and a lower box. In the upper box: Click on the 'Path' Variable and click Edit located on the right. Click New and paste your directory Path.

patterns.manual_review_disposition: Pre-flight diagnostic, not manual-review-gated. SGFX can run a PATH check before invoking pip and warn / offer to fix per Phase D auto-onboarding.
